comment_text.sn3.12:1.2: The five kings are not identified.
comment_text.sn3.12:2.2: This term, usually used by gods of the Buddha, is here used among kings to indicate equal status (see SN 1.1:1.4 and note).
comment_text.sn3.12:4.1: The Buddha could have simply rejected out of hand the idea that this was a worthwhile question. Instead, he educates the kings by proposing an analytical approach based in a psychology of empathy, encouraging the kings to remember that other people have different yet valid experiences.
comment_text.sn3.12:10.1: Candanaṅgalika is unknown elsewhere. The same verse, with a similar outcome, is spoken by the brahmin Piṅgīyānī at AN 5.195:3.1.
comment_text.sn3.12:11.3: Aṅgīras was one of the Vedic sages and the Aṅgīrasas are his descendants. They were priestly poets who assisted Indra in freeing the cows from the Vala cave, thus ushering in the dawn. Aṅgīras is regarded as the pravara or revered ancestor of the Gotama clan. Both names are associated with light: aṅgīrasa shares a root and is often synonymous with agni (“fire”), while gotama means “light dispelling dark”.

root_text.sn3.12:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena pañcannaṁ rājūnaṁ pasenadipamukhānaṁ pañcahi kāmaguṇehi samappitānaṁ samaṅgībhūtānaṁ paricārayamānānaṁ ayamantarākathā udapādi:
root_text.sn3.12:1.14: Yato kho te rājāno nāsakkhiṁsu aññamaññaṁ saññāpetuṁ.
root_text.sn3.12:2.1: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo te rājāno etadavoca:
root_text.sn3.12:2.2: “āyāma, mārisā, yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamissāma; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ etamatthaṁ paṭipucchissāma.
root_text.sn3.12:2.3: Yathā no bhagavā byākarissati tathā naṁ dhāressāmā”ti.
root_text.sn3.12:2.4: “Evaṁ, mārisā”ti kho te rājāno rañño pasenadissa kosalassa paccassosuṁ.
root_text.sn3.12:3.1: Atha kho te pañca rājāno pasenadipamukhā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho rājā pasenadi kosalo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn3.12:3.2: “idha, bhante, amhākaṁ pañcannaṁ rājūnaṁ pañcahi kāmaguṇehi samappitānaṁ samaṅgībhūtānaṁ paricārayamānānaṁ ayamantarākathā udapādi:
root_text.sn3.12:3.14: Kiṁ nu kho, bhante, kāmānaṁ aggan”ti?
root_text.sn3.12:4.1: “Manāpapariyantaṁ khvāhaṁ, mahārāja, pañcasu kāmaguṇesu agganti vadāmi.
root_text.sn3.12:4.2: Teva, mahārāja, rūpā ekaccassa manāpā honti, teva rūpā ekaccassa amanāpā honti.
root_text.sn3.12:4.3: Yehi ca yo rūpehi attamano hoti paripuṇṇasaṅkappo, so tehi rūpehi aññaṁ rūpaṁ uttaritaraṁ vā paṇītataraṁ vā na pattheti.
root_text.sn3.12:5.1: Teva, mahārāja, saddā ekaccassa manāpā honti, teva saddā ekaccassa amanāpā honti.
root_text.sn3.12:5.2: Yehi ca yo saddehi attamano hoti paripuṇṇasaṅkappo, so tehi saddehi aññaṁ saddaṁ uttaritaraṁ vā paṇītataraṁ vā na pattheti.
root_text.sn3.12:6.1: Teva, mahārāja, gandhā ekaccassa manāpā honti, teva gandhā ekaccassa amanāpā honti.
root_text.sn3.12:6.2: Yehi ca yo gandhehi attamano hoti paripuṇṇasaṅkappo, so tehi gandhehi aññaṁ gandhaṁ uttaritaraṁ vā paṇītataraṁ vā na pattheti.
root_text.sn3.12:7.1: Teva, mahārāja, rasā ekaccassa manāpā honti, teva rasā ekaccassa amanāpā honti.
root_text.sn3.12:7.2: Yehi ca yo rasehi attamano hoti paripuṇṇasaṅkappo, so tehi rasehi aññaṁ rasaṁ uttaritaraṁ vā paṇītataraṁ vā na pattheti.
root_text.sn3.12:8.1: Teva, mahārāja, phoṭṭhabbā ekaccassa manāpā honti, teva phoṭṭhabbā ekaccassa amanāpā honti.
root_text.sn3.12:8.2: Yehi ca yo phoṭṭhabbehi attamano hoti paripuṇṇasaṅkappo, so tehi phoṭṭhabbehi aññaṁ phoṭṭhabbaṁ uttaritaraṁ vā paṇītataraṁ vā na pattheti.
root_text.sn3.12:9.1: Tena kho pana samayena candanaṅgaliko upāsako tassaṁ parisāyaṁ nisinno hoti.
root_text.sn3.12:9.2: Atha kho candanaṅgaliko upāsako uṭṭhāyāsanā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā yena bhagavā tenañjaliṁ paṇāmetvā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn3.12:10.1: Atha kho candanaṅgaliko upāsako bhagavato sammukhā tadanurūpāya gāthāya abhitthavi:
root_text.sn3.12:12.1: Atha kho te pañca rājāno candanaṅgalikaṁ upāsakaṁ pañcahi uttarāsaṅgehi acchādesuṁ.
root_text.sn3.12:12.2: Atha kho candanaṅgaliko upāsako tehi pañcahi uttarāsaṅgehi bhagavantaṁ acchādesīti.

translation_text.sn3.12:1.2: Now at that time five kings headed by Pasenadi were amusing themselves, supplied and provided with the five kinds of sensual stimulation, and this discussion came up among them:
translation_text.sn3.12:1.5: “Sights are the best of sensual pleasures!”
translation_text.sn3.12:1.14: Since those kings were unable to persuade each other,
translation_text.sn3.12:2.2: “Come, good fellows, let’s go to the Buddha and ask him about this.
translation_text.sn3.12:2.3: As he answers, so we’ll remember it.”
translation_text.sn3.12:3.1: Then those five kings headed by Pasenadi went to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. King Pasenadi reported their conversation to the Buddha, and said,
translation_text.sn3.12:3.14: “Sir, what’s the best of sensual pleasures?”
translation_text.sn3.12:4.1: “Great king, which kind of sensual stimulation is best is defined by which is most agreeable, I say.
translation_text.sn3.12:4.2: The very same sights that are agreeable to some are disagreeable to others.
translation_text.sn3.12:4.3: When you’re happy with certain sights, as you’ve got all you wished for, you don’t want any other sight that’s better or finer.
translation_text.sn3.12:4.4: For you, those sights are perfect
translation_text.sn3.12:8.1: touches that are agreeable to some are disagreeable to others.
translation_text.sn3.12:8.2: When you’re happy with certain touches, as you’ve got all you wished for, you don’t want any other touch that’s better or finer.
translation_text.sn3.12:8.3: For you, those touches are perfect
translation_text.sn3.12:9.1: Now at that time the lay follower Candanaṅgalika was sitting in that assembly.
translation_text.sn3.12:9.2: Then he got up from his seat, arranged his robe over one shoulder, raised his joined palms toward the Buddha, and said,
translation_text.sn3.12:9.3: “I feel inspired to speak, Blessed One! I feel inspired to speak, Holy One!”
translation_text.sn3.12:9.4: “Then speak as you feel inspired,” said the Buddha.
translation_text.sn3.12:10.1: Then the lay follower Candanaṅgalika extolled the Buddha in his presence with an appropriate verse:
translation_text.sn3.12:11.2: that blooms at daybreak, its fragrance unfaded—
translation_text.sn3.12:11.4: bright as the sun in the sky!”
translation_text.sn3.12:12.1: Then those five kings clothed Candanaṅgalika with five upper robes.
translation_text.sn3.12:12.2: And Candanaṅgalika in turn endowed the Buddha with those robes.

variant_text.sn3.12:4.2: Teva → te ca (bj, pts1ed, pts2ed, mr); ye ca (sya-all, km) | teva → te ca (bj, sya-all, pts1ed, pts2ed, mr)

reference_text.sn3.12:1.1: bj13.150, csp1ed12.80, csp2ed12.80, ms12S1_640, msdiv123, ndp12.79, pts-vp-pli1ed1.80, pts-vp-pli2ed1.182, vri23.97
